skill.name: fastapi-pro
skill.description: Build production FastAPI services with async Python, SQLAlchemy 2.0, Pydantic V2, API security, testing, observability, and deployment guidance.
skill.summary: Guides production FastAPI API design, async data access, testing, security, and deployment.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported one external command pattern and eight weak cryptography patterns. Review found these are false positives from Markdown formatting and ordinary words containing the substring des. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, executable code, or confirmed dangerous behavior was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Resource Reference
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The text tells the assistant to open `resources/implementation-playbook.md` when examples are needed. It is Markdown formatting, not shell or Ruby backtick execution, and no command is executed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains a Markdown-formatted file path inside prose. There is no executable script, shell command, or interpolation context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The scanner appears to match the substring des in words such as description, Design, design, and Design API contracts. No weak cryptographic algorithm is configured or recommended on these lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited line is prose about API design or metadata. No line contains DES, MD5, SHA1, cipher configuration, or cryptographic implementation instructions.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent patterns for production FastAPI services. This skill guides async API design, data access, testing, security, and deployment decisions.
actual_capabilities: Designs REST, GraphQL, WebSocket, and SSE patterns for FastAPI applications.
Guides async SQLAlchemy 2.0, connection pooling, migrations, and repository patterns.
Plans authentication with OAuth2, JWT, API keys, RBAC, and security headers.
Creates testing strategies with pytest, pytest-asyncio, TestClient, mocks, and coverage.
Recommends observability with structured logging, OpenTelemetry, metrics, health checks, and APM tools.
Advises deployment with Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD, ASGI servers, and scaling strategies.
limitations: Provides guidance and implementation plans, not a hosted FastAPI runtime.
Cannot verify live infrastructure or database behavior without project access.
Does not replace security review for production authentication or authorization changes.
May require current dependency checks for FastAPI, Pydantic, and SQLAlchemy version changes.

use_cases.title: Design a New API Service
use_cases.description: Plan routes, schemas, dependencies, database access, validation, and tests for a new FastAPI service.

use_cases.title: Prepare Production Deployment
use_cases.description: Review ASGI server settings, containers, health checks, logging, metrics, and scaling plans before launch.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing API Performance
use_cases.description: Identify async bottlenecks, connection pool issues, caching opportunities, pagination problems, and slow database queries.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic API Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a small FastAPI service.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a FastAPI service for [domain]. Include routes, Pydantic models, dependencies, error handling, and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Add Async Database Access
prompt_templates.prompt: Design async SQLAlchemy 2.0 data access for this FastAPI app. Include sessions, repositories, migrations, transactions, and test strategy.

prompt_templates.title: Secure a Production API
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my FastAPI authentication plan. Cover OAuth2, JWT refresh tokens, RBAC, API keys, CORS, rate limits, and security headers.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Scaled Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this FastAPI architecture for production scale. Focus on async bottlenecks, caching, pooling, observability, deployments, and failure handling.

output_examples.input: Design a FastAPI service for order management.
output: A route map for orders, customers, payments, and status changes.
A schema plan using Pydantic V2 validation and response models.
A test plan covering happy paths, validation errors, and authorization failures.
A prioritized list of missing health checks, metrics, logging, and deployment controls.
Recommendations for ASGI workers, connection pools, request timeouts, and graceful shutdown.
Security review points for CORS, tokens, rate limits, and error responses.

output_examples.input: Optimize a slow endpoint with database access.
output: Likely causes including blocking calls, N+1 queries, missing indexes, and oversized responses.
A step-by-step profiling plan with metrics to collect before changing code.
Targeted improvements for eager loading, pagination, caching, and connection reuse.

best_practices: Start with clear API contracts, Pydantic models, and error behavior before implementation.
Use async libraries end to end so request handlers do not block the event loop.
Add tests, observability, and deployment checks before treating an API as production ready.
anti_patterns: Mixing blocking database or HTTP clients into async request handlers.
Adding authentication without explicit token lifetimes, refresh behavior, and authorization rules.
Optimizing performance before measuring latency, query counts, and resource usage.

faq.question: What FastAPI versions does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets modern FastAPI 0.100 and later, with Pydantic V2 and current async Python practices.

faq.question: Can it help with SQLAlchemy 2.0?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers async sessions, repositories, transactions, migrations, pooling, and query optimization.

faq.question: Does it generate complete application code?
faq.answer: It can guide implementation and produce structured plans, but project details determine final code.

faq.question: Can it review API security?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers OAuth2, JWT, API keys, RBAC, CORS, rate limits, validation, and security headers.

faq.question: Is it useful for production deployment?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD, ASGI server tuning, health checks, metrics, and scaling guidance.

faq.question: Can it help with WebSockets?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports FastAPI WebSocket design, real-time communication patterns, and scaling considerations.
